question_text: In a series circuit, a battery is connected to two bulbs. If one bulb breaks and the circuit is broken, what happens to the other bulb?

options.label: A
options.text: It continues to light at full brightness

options.label: B
options.text: It stops lighting because the circuit is broken

options.label: C
options.text: It lights more brightly because there is less resistance

options.label: D
options.text: It flashes on and off randomly

explanation: In a series circuit, all components are connected in one loop. When one bulb breaks, it breaks the complete path for current to flow, so the whole circuit stops working and the other bulb goes out.
